description_long: This lamp features a cluster of five downward-facing shades, each resembling a bell or tulip, suspended from a central canopy by individual cords. The shades are semi-transparent, likely made of frosted or textured glass, allowing diffused light to pass through while concealing the bulbs inside. The cords converge at a decorative, flared canopy that attaches to the ceiling, providing both structural support and a visual focal point. The overall design is elegant and modern, with a soft, organic silhouette that would complement contemporary or transitional urban interiors. The lamp's structure suggests it is intended to hang from above, distributing light evenly below. Subtle decorative ridges or patterns are visible on the shades, adding a touch of sophistication.
description: A ceiling-mounted pendant lamp with five frosted glass shades, each suspended by a cord from a central canopy. The design is modern and elegant, featuring soft, organic lines and subtle decorative details.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five bell-shaped shades evenly spaced and the central canopy above.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the depth and curvature of the shades, with cords more visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the symmetrical arrangement of the shades and canopy.
description_view_3: Another angled view highlighting the overlapping shades and the convergence of cords at the canopy.
